title: bromus diandrus var. madritensis
intro: Bromus diandrus var. madritensis, also known as Spanish brome, is a subspecies of the wild oat grass, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of Spanish brome can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
